title: 'Raped another day before': Killer knew IRS officer's routine, timed entry to assault daughter
content: A 23-year-old man, accused of a brutal rape and murder in Delhi, allegedly committed another heinous crime in Alwar, Rajasthan, hours prior. He forced his way into a woman's home, raped and attempted to strangle her before fleeing. Police apprehended him in a Delhi hotel after a multi-state manhunt.

title: ‘I don’t have answers… no excuses’: Rishabh Pant after LSG’s crushing defeat
content: Rishabh Pant expressed deep disappointment after Lucknow Super Giants' batting collapse, admitting his team "had no answers" in their 40-run IPL 2026 defeat. He called for introspection, stating the batting unit, including himself, failed to adapt to the challenging pitch.  Rajasthan Royals, anchored by Ravindra Jadeja's unbeaten 43, successfully defended their modest total.

title: ‘Bush, Obama said no’: Trump agreed to Netanyahu's war plan after rejections by 3 US Presidents
content: John Kerry revealed that Benjamin Netanyahu pitched an Iran war to four US Presidents, with only Donald Trump agreeing. Kerry, citing Vietnam and Iraq, warned against misleading the public on military action. A New York Times report detailed Netanyahu's "hard sell" to Trump for a joint US-Israeli mission, which Trump reportedly approved.

title: 'He may be underestimating': Nobel-winner economist on Anthropic CEO’s jobs wipeout claim
content: Nobel laureate Daron Acemoglu challenges Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei's prediction of mass entry-level job elimination due to AI. Acemoglu argues that many white-collar roles are too complex for current AI, and job displacement depends on adoption and new job creation. He urges policymakers to prepare for potential social unrest if AI exacerbates inequality.

title: KSEAB K'taka SSLC Class 10 result: Check website list, steps to download from DigiLocker
content: Karnataka Class 10 results are out today, April 23rd, at noon. Nearly 9 lakh students registered for the SSLC exams, with over 8 lakh appearing.  Candidates can access their marks online via official KSEAB websites, the KarnatakaOne app, or SMS.  Digital marksheets will also be available on DigiLocker from noon onwards.  Last year's pass rate stood at 66.14%.

title: After $6.25 bn 'gift' to 'Trump Accounts', Michael & Susan Dell donate $750 m
content: Michael and Susan Dell have pledged $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin for a new medical center and research campus. This significant donation will focus on integrating AI and advanced computing to improve patient care, research, and education. The project, expected to open by 2030, aims to enhance Austin's healthcare capabilities and advance medical innovation.

title: ISI tactic? Punjab woman becomes 'Sarabnoor', invites Sikhs to visit Pakistan in video
content: Sarabjit Kaur, who converted to Islam and married a Pakistani man, has released a new video from Pakistan inviting Sikhs to visit the country. This conciliatory message follows earlier provocative remarks, raising suspicions among Indian intelligence agencies who believe she may be acting under ISI influence to gauge Sikh public reaction.

title: 'Bhooth Bangla' box office Day 6: Akshay Kumar starrer crosses Rs 127 crore globally
content: Akshay Kumar's latest horror-comedy, 'Bhooth Bangla', is making waves at the box office, raking in over Rs 100 crore globally within its first four days. Although it faced a slight decline during the weekdays, the film still attracted audiences, collecting Rs 6.15 crore on Day 6 in India and pushing its total net earnings to Rs 78.90 crore. Read on to know more in detail.

title: 'We are installing tracking software in your machines': Meta to US employees
content: Meta is installing new tracking software on US employees' computers to capture mouse movements, clicks, and keystrokes for AI training. This initiative aims to improve AI agents' ability to perform work tasks autonomously, like using shortcuts. Meta assures employees the data won't be used for performance reviews, only for enhancing AI models.

title: Kollywood stars cast votes: Rajinikanth, Vijay, and Ajith lead early polling push
content: The team behind Suriya's upcoming film 'Karuppu' has arrived in Sri Lanka. They received a grand welcome. The cast and crew are there for film promotions and to complete shooting. This overseas schedule is generating significant buzz. The movie is set for a May 14 release. Expectations are high for this RJ Balaji directorial.

title: Watch | ‘Dimaag me kuch nahi chalta’: Sooryavanshi reveals his pre-match routine
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi became the youngest player to reach 500 IPL runs, achieving the milestone in just 227 balls. Despite a quiet 8 runs against Lucknow Super Giants, his record-breaking feat and admission of watching cartoons before matches captivated fans. Rajasthan Royals secured a 40-run victory, with Ravindra Jadeja earning Player of the Match.

title: UP man strangled by wife, children over fear of losing daughter’s Rs 82.5k wedding fund
content: A man was allegedly murdered by his wife, daughters, and minor son in Mahoba district. The family feared he would misuse Rs 82,500 arranged for his daughter's wedding. The body was found in a drain. The accused confessed to strangling him and dumping the body. Police recovered the murder weapon, cash, and a damaged mobile phone.

title: Explained: Why China’s AI computing power looks 6,000x bigger
content: China reports a staggering 1,882 exaflops of AI computing power, vastly exceeding global benchmarks and raising questions about hidden capacity. While metrics differ, adjusted figures still show significant advancement. This development, coupled with China's withdrawal from global rankings amid geopolitical tensions, suggests a rapidly narrowing gap with the US in AI infrastructure and capability.

title: Who was Amal Khalil? Lebanese journalist killed as Israel–Hezbollah tensions escalate
content: A Lebanese journalist, Amal Khalil, was tragically killed in an Israeli airstrike in southern Lebanon while covering the ongoing conflict. Rescue efforts were significantly delayed by Israeli fire, preventing immediate access to the site. Khalil's death, the ninth journalist killed this year, has drawn condemnation and calls for international law adherence.

title: Viral 7-day diet plan: Know the weekly meals that helped fitness influencer lose 20 kgs
content: Weight loss advice in 2026 is surprisingly simple. A viral 7-day Indian meal plan focuses on whole foods and portion control. This realistic approach is gaining attention for its balance and consistency. Scientific research supports each day's nutritional logic. The plan emphasises everyday home-cooked meals and sustainable habits over extreme diets. It offers a doable strategy for long-term success.

title: BCCI pushes for Jasprit Bumrah to play entire WTC cycle
content: Following their T20 World Cup win, selectors are prioritising the World Test Championship and 2027 ODI World Cup, focusing on managing Jasprit Bumrah's workload. Bumrah is expected to play all nine upcoming WTC Tests, with potential rest from ODIs. Middle-order prospects like Padikkal, Jurel, and Gaikwad are also being considered.

title: ‘Raped another woman in Rajasthan’: Chilling details emerge in IRS officer’s daughter murder case
content: A 22-year-old daughter of a senior bureaucrat was allegedly raped and murdered at her south Delhi residence. Police arrested a 23-year-old former domestic help within 12 hours, who was dismissed for financial misconduct. He is accused of robbery, sexual assault, and murder, and is also wanted for a similar crime in Rajasthan.

title: Youngest and fastest! Sooryavanshi scripts history, breaks two major IPL records
content: Teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi made history by becoming the youngest player to reach 500 Indian Premier League runs and the fastest by balls faced. Despite a quiet 8 runs, his achievement came during Rajasthan Royals' 40-run victory over Lucknow Super Giants. Ravindra Jadeja's all-round performance secured the win for RR.

title: Pentagon shake-up continues: US navy secretary steps down abruptly amid war with Iran
content: US Navy secretary John Phelan has abruptly resigned, with Hung Cao stepping in as acting chief. This departure occurs amidst heightened regional tensions, including the Navy's blockade of Iranian ports and ongoing operations in critical waterways. Phelan's exit is the latest in a series of significant leadership changes within the Pentagon.

title: 'Playing at Perth': Justin Langer compares Ekana to WACA as LSG struggle at home
content: Lucknow Super Giants coach Justin Langer admits his team struggles with the Ekana Stadium's pace and bounce, comparing it to Perth's WACA.  Despite a brilliant surface, LSG batters haven't adapted, leading to three home losses. Langer praised his bowlers, highlighting Mohsin Khan and Prince Yadav as future India stars.

title: Oil prices today: Crude edges lower as Iran–US tensions keep markets on edge
content: Oil prices saw a slight dip after Wednesday's surge, as stalled Iran-US peace talks and ongoing shipping route restrictions, particularly the Strait of Hormuz, kept markets on edge. Iran's seizure of two ships and US interception of tankers highlight escalating tensions. Despite record US energy exports, domestic crude inventories unexpectedly rose, while fuel stocks declined.

title: 'Vote in record numbers': PM Modi reminds voters of their 'sacred duty' as TN, Bengal go to polls
content: Prime Minister Modi urged voters in West Bengal and Tamil Nadu to participate in the "sacred duty of democracy" as polling commenced for crucial Assembly elections. Appealing particularly to young voters and women, he emphasized the importance of their participation in this significant electoral exercise.

title: 8 clever interior design tricks to make small bedroom feel like luxury space
content: Small bedrooms can feel larger with smart design. Experts suggest using exhibition techniques for better flow and zoning. Layered lighting and multi-functional furniture are key. Curating items and using vertical space also helps. Engaging senses and refreshing the space regularly creates a comfortable retreat. These methods offer a new perspective on maximising limited space.

title: ‘MS Dhoni will only play when…’- CSK coach breaks silence ahead of crucial MI clash
content: Chennai Super Kings face uncertainty regarding MS Dhoni's return for IPL 2026 due to a persistent calf injury. Despite recent training, his comeback remains unconfirmed, with the team also grappling with other player injuries. This has impacted CSK's performance, placing them eighth on the table ahead of a crucial match against a confident Mumbai Indians.

title: ‘Dhurandhar 2’ marches slowly towards Rs 1770 cr worldwide
content: Ranveer Singh's spy thriller 'Dhurandhar 2' has crossed Rs 1,764 crore worldwide after a 35-day run. Despite initial dominance, Akshay Kumar's 'Bhooth Bangla' has now emerged as a strong competitor. 'Dhurandhar 2' continues its steady climb, aiming for Rs 1770 crore by the weekend, showcasing impressive longevity at the box office.

title: IPL: Jofra Archer creates history, goes past Yuzvendra Chahal to become…
content: Jofra Archer achieved a historic milestone for Rajasthan Royals. He became the franchise's leading IPL wicket-taker with a stellar bowling performance. Archer's spell secured a crucial victory for his team. Rajasthan bowlers defended a low total successfully. This win highlights the team's bowling strength. Archer's achievement marks his significant impact.

title: 27 lakh under scrutiny, 139 cleared to vote in Bengal Phase 1 polls
content: As Bengal gears up for its assembly elections, tribunals have restored voting privileges to 139 individuals whose names were previously expunged during a meticulous revision process. This reinstatement, albeit affecting just a small segment of the over 27 lakh originally disqualified voters, marks a positive step.

title: Ex-factor decoded: Bengaluru woman who set lover ablaze says boyfriend avoided her
content: A Bengaluru woman, Prema, allegedly murdered her boyfriend, Kiran, out of jealousy after discovering he reconnected with his ex-girlfriend. She lured him into a BDSM role-play at her residence before carrying out the fatal act. Prema confessed to the crime, stating she planned to prevent him from marrying anyone else.

title: Dalit groom pulled off horse, beaten during pre-wedding procession in Madhya Pradesh
content: In a shocking incident that unfolded during a pre-wedding procession in Damoh, Madhya Pradesh, a Dalit groom was violently confronted and dragged off his horse. Four individuals have been apprehended on charges of caste-based violence, facing severe repercussions under the National Security Act.

title: West Bengal Elections 2026 LIVE: Clashes in Murshidabad, Humayun Kabir faces 'go back' slogans in Naoda; Bengal records 18.76% voting as of 9 a.m.
content: Several voters in Domkal alleged that they were threatened and intimidated, with some claiming they were warned of assault if they attempted to reach polling stations

content: Humayun Kabir was met with protests from TMC supporters who raised "go back" slogans when the leader visited a polling booth in Shibnagar village in Murshidabad's Naoda Assembly constituency.

content: According to police, the victim was discovered by her family members inside the house under suspicious circumstances, following which the police were alerted.

content: According to preliminary findings, a truck travelling downhill is suspected to have suffered a brake failure before ramming into another truck moving ahead of it

content: The incident at Chirayinkeezhu has evoked the grim memory of another child, Aljo Siljo, who died after being bitten by a snake while sleeping in Thrissur recently

title: Maharashtra bypolls: 8% turnout in Baramati, 6% in Rahuri till 9 a.m.

content: Justice Karia, a former partner at a leading law firm, represented Meta in several cases before his elevation as a judge

content: They identified the OGWs as Zahoor Ahmad Mir, Bashir Ahmad Bhat, and Ghulam Mohammad Bhat, all residents of Hazratbal.

content: Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) Chief Vijay cast his vote at Vaels International School in Chennai on April 23, 2026.

content: INDIA bloc leaders, including J&K CM Omar Abdullah, Samajwadi Party president Akhilesh Yadav, and RJD leader Tejashwi Yadav have been in touch with Mamata Banerjee and TMC leaders; AAP convenor Arvind Kejriwal extends support to the West Bengal CM

content: Seizures include ₹127.67 crore in cash, nearly 41,000 litres of liquor worth ₹106.3 crore, drugs worth nearly ₹184 crore, precious metals worth ₹215 crore, and freebies worth ₹418 crore

title: Chief Minister walking into an ongoing ED raid is not a Centre-State dispute, Supreme Court says
content: The ED would commence its rejoinder arguments on April 23, following which the court is expected to reserve the case for orders

title: Assembly Elections 2026: Know the documents required for voting
content: If you are voting in these elections, it is important to know the documents that you can use to cast your vote on the day of polling.

content: BJP claims the Congress chief has violated Model Code of Conduct and says such “rhetoric lowers the level of campaign discourse, inflamed passions, and undermined free and fair elections”

title: Pahalgam terror attack: Govt appoints Karambir Singh Nalwa as Special Public Prosecutor to lead trial
content: The NIA had filed a comprehensive chargesheet against six people and two terrorist organisations, Pakistan-based Lashkar-e-Taiba and its shadow outfit TRF, in connection with the terror attack

title: Order to register FIR in K.N. Nehru cash-for-jobs case has errors, Tamil Nadu govt. tells Madras High Court
content: ED will exercise complete dominance over State powers if police were mandated to register FIRs based on every other informatiion shared by the central agency, the State contends in its review plea

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: A two-cornered contest in the AIADMK stronghold Pappireddipatti constituency
content: Predominantly agrarian, one of the primary demands has been implementation of irrigation schemes to support agriculture, and setting up of agro-based industries to help marketing the produce.

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Development gaps and heritage identity shape Jayankondam constituency’s electoral narrative
content: The constituency reflects a blend of historical significance and present-day concerns, where issues such as tourism development, livelihood, infrastructure gaps and employment opportunities are expected to shape voter choices in the upcoming polls.

title: Baramati, Rahuri all set to conduct byelections on April 23
content: The Baramati bypoll holds significance, as these will be the first elections since the death of the eight-time MLA from the constituency, Ajit Pawar

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Is the ground under PMK shifting to favour TVK in Dharmapuri constituency?
content: The constituency is regarded as a PMK stronghold and a ‘safe-seat’ for Ms. Sowmiya. However, the ground in this PMK’s ‘safe-seat’ appears to be shifting  in favour of the TVK.

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Leemarose trying her luck in DMK’s pocketborough Lalgudi constituency
content: In the 12 Assembly elections held since 1971,  the DMK won the seat eight times; the constituency is blessed with rich groundwater resource making it one of the most fertile regions in Cauvery delta

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Uthangarai constituency braces for a two-cornered contest
content: A water harvesting project from Paambaru Dam envisioning a watershed of 4,000 acres, an irrigation project tapping into the run-off from KRP Dam to fill up 34 lakes in Barur are among the two significant demands of farmers, where farming is affected by drought.

title: SRMIST organises international conference on advancements in electrical and electronic tech
content: The international conference was held on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication and Computational Intelligence

title: Over 12,000 police personnel to be deployed on polling day in Vellore, nearby districts
content: Election officials said that the police in these districts have roped in personnel from all wings, including the Armed Reserve, law and order and traffic

title: Over ₹78 lakh in cash seized from DMK candidate’s aide in Mylapore
content: Officials also seized written instructions allegedly related to distribution of money to the party’s local functionaries, and a cash-counting machine

title: Suresh Babu assumes office as Chairman & Managing Director of BHAVINI
content: BHAVINI is responsible for the construction, commissioning, operation and maintenance of the 500MWe Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor in Kalpakkam and future Fast Breeder Reactors

title: Madras High Court denies permission for construction of multi-storey apartments at Parthasarathy Gardens in Alwarpet
content: Justice V. Lakshminarayanan quashes planning permission granted by Chennai Metropolitan Development Authority to RUIA Developers LLP

title: Demand to augment comfortable public transport buses along NH 66 to reduce dependance on private vehicles
content: Four/six-laning of NH 66 nears completion as demand grows to augment bus connectivity; experts call for more KSRTC buses, private participation, and a public transport policy to lessen reliance on private vehicles

title: Pilot project to plug leaks fails to reassure residents; P&T Apartment complex in Mundamveli continue to leak
content: Two coats of a leak-proof solution applied in one bathroom for a 10-day pilot; residents say it addresses only bathroom leaks, not rainwater seepage caused by structural flaws

title: Amaravati expansion gains pace with fresh land pooling, acquisition notices
content: State issues LPS and LAAR Act notifications in Guntur and Palnadu districts, focusing on infrastructure, flood mitigation and future airport plans

title: Samrat Choudhary approves funds for 75 ITI in first Cabinet meeting as Bihar CM
content: 75 ITI institutions in the state will be developed as state-of-the-art skill training centres; students will be taught modern, in-demand courses such as AI, semiconductors, sales and retail

title: Man killed in Bengaluru in dispute over pet pigeon
content: A 25-year-old private firm employee was allegedly murdered following a quarrel over a pigeon in Rajagopalanagar

title: Mahanadi tribunal gives last opportunity to Odisha, Chhattisgarh to settle river water dispute
content: The MWDT observed that no concrete proposal for settlement on any of the disputed issues was placed on record by two States

title: Woman who set male friend on fire in police custody in Bengaluru
content: A day after a man was burnt alive, the Byadarahlli police have taken Prema, 27, his female friend, into three days custody to ascertain the exact motive behind the murder

title: Panel set up to probe death at Bengaluru’s Victoria Hospital following ‘negligence’ allegation
content: A probe has been ordered at the State-run Victoria Hospital following allegations by a patient’s family that medical negligence led to his death

title: Party support for Independent candidate, an election strategy in which Jayalalithaa succeeded and MGR failed
content: Tamil Nadu has in the past witnessed two elections in which Independent candidates secured the backing of a mainstream political party — with strikingly different results. The two instances were separated by nearly 25 years

title: Former DGP R. Sreelekha’s protest slogan sparks debate
content: Several social media users point to the irony of a former police officer raising slogans against the force, while others criticised the language, which was deemed disrespectful

title: Bengaluru’s Yellow, Pink metro lines to spur realty prices: Report
content: These two metro lines are expected to spur real estate development across major micro markets; 5-7 million sq.ft incremental office demand is anticipated over the next two years in CBD, SBD 2 and Electronic City areas, predicts Colliers India

content: Allegations of voter intimidation have surfaced from Murshidabad and Trinamool workers clashed with Humayun Kabir in the same district as West Bengal votes in phase 1.

content: West Bengal Assembly Elections 2026 Phase 1 Voting LIVE Updates: Around 3.6 crore voters in 152 of the state's 294 constituencies are expected to vote today.

title: Teen Gives Birth Inside Toilet Of Apple Factory In Bengaluru, Slits Throat
content: Renuka, an employee at the Foxconn factory in Devanahalli, delivered the baby inside a toilet and then slit his throat and dumped the body in a bag.

title: NTK's Senthamizan Seeman Continues His Solo Push In Tamil Nadu
content: The 59-year-old actor-turned-politician has emerged as a lone political figure focused on Tamil nationalism, language pride and identity.

content: The video then uses a SpongeBob SquarePants '2,000 years later' meme template to show AI Trump getting frustrated at Iran's no-show.

content: The pilot wanted to capture footage of his last sortie before reassignment, according to the report by the Board of Audit and Inspection.

content: Tamil Nadu is voting today for the assembly elections, with the high-stakes contest pitting the DMK alliance against the AIADMK-led NDA. MK Stalin, whose campaign centred around the "Dravidian Model" of governance, is seeking to retain power.

title: West Bengal, Tamil Nadu Elections 2026 LIVE Updates: Voting Begins
content: West Bengal, Tamil Nadu Election Voting 2026 LIVE Updates: Around 3.6 crore voters in 152 of Bengal's 294 constituencies across 16 districts are expected to vote in the first phase today.

title: Trinamool-BJP Battle Royale In Bengal With 152 Seats In Phase 1
content: Two states that have eluded the BJP for long are voting today and more than Tamil Nadu, it is Bengal that all eyes will be on. For Trinamool Congress and the BJP, it is a desperate battle amid the political firestorm over voter list revision.

title: Trump Fires Top US Navy Official, Reports Cites "Bad Relations" With Hegseth
content: The Pentagon announced US Navy Secretary John Phelan's departure in a brief statement, saying he was leaving the administration "effective immediately."

content: US-Iran War, Ceasefire, Peace Talks Updates: The war is in its eighth week and has sent oil prices surging because of the closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Man Arrested After 14 Years On Run In 2012 Delhi Robbery-Abduction Case
content: Delhi Police have arrested a Rs 20,000 rewardee and proclaimed offender who had been evading capture for 14 years in connection with a 2012 robbery-cum-abduction case in Dwarka South, officials said Wednesday.

title: 1 In 4 Stock Market Investors Is Now A Woman, Demat Accounts Jump By 129%
content: As many as 19 states now report female investor participation above the national average. More than 3 crore demat accounts are operated by women.

title: Structures Linked To Criminal On Government Land Demolished In Gurugram
content: The Gurugram police on Wednesday demolished illegal structures on government land in Kasan village allegedly linked to a criminal, as part of an ongoing drive to dismantle criminals' financial networks, officials said.

title: Dog Bites About 40 People In Madhya Pradesh
content: A dog bit nearly 40 people all of whom are being treated at the Satna District Hospital here, an official said on Wednesday.

title: Amravati sex scandal: Ayaan Ahmed paraded through village after videos of minors recovered on phone
content: police paraded the main accused, Ayaan Ahmed, for 600m from police station . they recovered a phone which has now been sent for forensic analysis . police are investigating details of a Thar SUV and "pistols" seen in a reel . the owner of the Thar has been identified as Rajesh Prajapati .

content: Kash Patel breaks silence on his video of chugging beer and says he's never drunk on the job. title: Security ramped up as Tamil Nadu goes to polls; battle for 234 seats
content: 300 companies of paramilitary forces and more than 83,800 police personnel have been deployed to guard polling stations . EC officials undertook a 65km uphill journey from Theni to move ballot units to a remote polling booth under the Andipatti assembly segment .

title: Congress gets nod for Rahul Gandhi's rallies in West Bengal

title: 'Bodol noy, bodla chai': Didi flips 2011 election slogan
content: Mamata Banerjee gave a "bodol noy, bodla chai (revenge, not change)" call in 2011 . she demolished the decades-old left front fort in the state with historic win . "we will not allow Bengal to be set on fire like Lanka," she says .
